target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix: recover stranded replies in message_tool_only mode (#85714)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.visibleReplies` is set to `message_tool`, the agent is expected to call the message tool to deliver replies. If the LLM forgets (a probabilistic event — the reporter saw it happen on the 8th turn after 7 correct deliveries), the reply was **silently discarded**: the substantive text sat in the JSONL transcript but was never delivered to the user.

The existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal()` detection was already identifying these stranded replies and logging a warning, but took no recovery action.

## Fix

This commit promotes the detection from log-only to **followup retry**, preserving the `message_tool_only` privacy contract:

1. **Gate room events**: Skip stranded reply detection for ambient `room_event` turns where `message_tool_only` is automatically set by the system. Silence on room events is intentional by design — the agent chose not to respond.

2. **Enqueue followup retry turn**: When a stranded reply is detected, enqueue a followup run whose prompt instructs the agent to deliver the captured text via `message(action=send)`. The agent itself calls the message tool, so delivery happens through the correct contract path — no suppression bypass, no privacy boundary crossing.

3. **Clear transcript/persistence fields**: The retry run explicitly clears `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` from the original followup run to prevent the original user prompt from hiding the retry instruction. The followup runner resolves `transcriptPrompt ?? extracted.text`, so a retained `transcriptPrompt` would cause the agent to see the original user question instead of the retry delivery instruction.

4. **No suppression bypass**: This does NOT mark payloads with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` or use `skipSuppressionCheck`. The `message_tool_only` privacy contract is fully preserved: delivery only happens when the agent explicitly calls `message(action=send)`.

Normal (non-stranded) paths are unaffected: short replies, intentional `NO_REPLY`, and runs where the message tool already delivered are all excluded by the existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal` guard.

### Why followup retry instead of bypass delivery?

The `message_tool_only` contract says: "no message tool call = private/silent". Auto-publishing stranded text would break this documented behavior. Instead, we give the agent a second chance to follow the contract correctly — like returning an undelivered letter and asking the sender to mail it properly, rather than opening and publishing it ourselves.

## Changed files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` — followup retry with cleaned transcript fields, room_event gate (+26/-3 net)
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` — 5 new retry/gate tests replacing 6 bypass tests (-4 net)

## Tests

| Suite | Count | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| `agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` | 47 | ✅ All pass |
| `private-message-tool-final.test.ts` | 8 | ✅ All pass |
| `dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | 189 | ✅ All pass (unchanged, confirms no regressions) |
| `followup-runner.test.ts` | 67 | ✅ All pass (confirms followup queue compatibility) |

New test cases:
- `enqueues a followup retry for a stranded substantive reply (#85714)` — verifies `enqueueFollowupRun` is called with retry prompt containing the assistant text
- `does not enqueue retry for short private final replies` — negative case
- `does not enqueue retry when the message tool delivered this turn` — negative case
- `does not enqueue retry for room_event turns (#85714)` — verifies room_event gate
- `clears transcriptPrompt on retry to prevent original prompt from hiding retry instruction (#85714)` — regression test verifying `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` are all `undefined` on the retry run

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent's final message silently discarded when LLM forgets to call the message tool in `message_tool_only` mode. The fix enqueues a followup retry turn so the agent can deliver via `message(action=send)`, preserving the privacy contract.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0, patched checkout at `9dc3c76dc3`.
- **Privacy contract preserved**: The fix does NOT use `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` or `skipSuppressionCheck`. No payload is auto-delivered. The agent is re-prompted to call `message(action=send)` itself.
- **Transcript isolation**: The retry run clears `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` so the followup runner's `transcriptPrompt ?? extracted.text` resolution correctly uses the retry prompt, not the original user message.
- **Room event safety**: Stranded reply detection is gated by `sessionCtx.InboundEventKind !== "room_event"`, so ambient room events (where silence is intentional) never trigger recovery. Covered by dedicated test case.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, `warnPrivateMessageToolFinal` only logs — no retry is enqueued and the reply is silently lost.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  ✓ auto-reply agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts (47 tests) 1729ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  47 passed (47)

  ✓ unit-fast private-message-tool-final.test.ts (8 tests) 2ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  8 passed (8)

  ✓ auto-reply dispatch-from-config.test.ts (189 tests) 3770ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  189 passed (189)

  ✓ auto-reply followup-runner.test.ts (67 tests) 1710ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  67 passed (67)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: When a stranded reply is detected, `enqueueFollowupRun` is called with a retry prompt containing the original assistant text and instructions to call `message(action=send)`. The retry run has `transcriptPrompt: undefined` so the followup runner correctly uses the retry instruction. Short replies, already-delivered replies, room events, and runs with existing transcript prompts are all verified by dedicated test cases.
- **What was not tested**: Live end-to-end delivery through an actual channel — the test verifies that the correct followup run is enqueued with the expected prompt shape and cleaned fields. The followup queue machinery (`enqueueFollowupRun` → `scheduleFollowupDrain` → `runReplyAgent`) is a well-tested existing mechanism used for concurrent message queuing.

Closes #85714

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (includes all 12 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **55 tests passed** (284ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (55 tests) 284ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **10 tests passed** (63ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 63ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 13ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1000 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1000 passed (1002)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 new regression tests pass, confirming that manual runs preserve one-shot jobs (`deleteAfterRun`), do not increment/reset counters, do not disable at-jobs on error, do not rewrite recurring `nextRunAtMs` with backoff, and do not clear failure-alert cooldown. Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **11 tests passed** (92ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (11 tests) 92ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 10ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job → NOT deleted ✅
  - Manual error → counters NOT incremented ✅
  - Manual success → counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared ✅
  - Manual at-job error → NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual every-job error → backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped at-job → consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` → failure alert NOT emitted ✅

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`, 2026-06-02)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `lastRunWasManual` field was missing from the `CronJobStateSchema` and `CronJobStatePatchSchema` in `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/cron.ts`. Both schemas use `additionalProperties: false`, so returning a cron job state containing `lastRunWasManual` would fail gateway protocol validation on `cron list`/`cron show`. Added `lastRunWasManual: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean())` to both schemas.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`), Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `ccb89e3f11`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On the previous commit (`45efd1abdd`), `CronJobStateSchema` did not include `lastRunWasManual`. Any cron job state object with `lastRunWasManual: true` (set by `timer.ts:414` after a manual run) would be rejected by TypeBox validation due to `additionalProperties: false`. The `CronJobStatePatchSchema` was similarly missing the field, preventing state patches that include it.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" \
  node:24-bookworm \
  bash -c '
    cd /app && npm install 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
      src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts 2>&1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 335ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 54ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 31ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts (3 tests) 3ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts (2 tests) 0ms

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  86 passed (86)
   Start at  07:37:28
   Duration  3.44s (transform 2.31s, setup 280ms, import 2.62s, tests 424ms, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 86 tests pass including:
  - `timer.regression.test.ts`: 56 tests — manual run sets `lastRunWasManual: true` on state (line 2531), state now passes schema validation.
  - `service.restart-catchup.test.ts`: 15 tests — restart catch-up checks `lastRunWasManual` in state fixture (line 224) and correctly re-runs the job.
  - `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`: 3 tests — schema conformance verified (delivery modes, status shape, failover reasons).
  - `cron-protocol-schema.test.ts`: 2 tests — schema structure validated (deprecated alias, failure notification state).

- **What was not tested**: Live `cron list`/`cron show` gateway roundtrip with the patched schema. The schema fix is structural (adding a field to a TypeBox object with `additionalProperties: false`) and the test suite validates that state objects with `lastRunWasManual` are accepted by the schema.

## Live Gateway CLI roundtrip proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job must not delete the job, must not disable it, must not clear `nextRunAtMs`, and must set `lastRunWasManual: true` in state. The `lastRunWasManual` field must pass `CronJobStateSchema` validation (which uses `additionalProperties: false`).
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64, OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, OpenClaw Gateway built from source at commit `ccb89e3f11`.
- **Steps**:
  1. Built OpenClaw from source on the PR branch
  2. Started Gateway inside Docker with `gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth token`
  3. Created a `deleteAfterRun` at-job via `cron add`
  4. Manually ran the job via `cron run` (resulted in error due to missing codex-linux-arm64 in Docker — this is the interesting case: manual error on a deleteAfterRun job)
  5. Verified job survival, `lastRunWasManual: true`, `enabled: true`, and `nextRunAtMs` preserved via `cron show` and `cron list`
  6. Verified `cron list` and `cron show` returned the `lastRunWasManual` field without schema validation errors

<details>

- **Observed result**: After manual `cron run` (which triggered an error due to missing codex dependency in Docker):
  - Job **survived** — still present in `cron list` and `cron show` ✅
  - `enabled: true` — manual error did NOT disable the at-job ✅
  - `nextRunAtMs: 1798732799000` — preserved, NOT cleared ✅
  - `deleteAfterRun: true` — still set, NOT consumed by manual run ✅
  - `lastRunWasManual: true` — new field present and accepted by schema ✅
  - No `consecutiveErrors` field — manual error did NOT increment counter ✅
  - Run history shows `runId: "manual:..."` prefix confirming manual execution path ✅
  - Both `cron show` and `cron list` returned `lastRunWasManual` without schema validation errors, proving `CronJobStateSchema` correctly includes the field ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` (would require a working LLM provider in Docker). The `shouldDelete` guard path (`wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual`) is exercised by unit tests. Multi-node cluster, production load.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 — backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` — blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Unit tests in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c \
  "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts"

# Live Gateway CLI in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  -e OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-test \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=test-token-123 \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c '
    mkdir -p /tmp/openclaw-test
    echo "{\"gateway\":{\"port\":19001,\"bind\":\"loopback\"}}" > /tmp/openclaw-test/openclaw.json
    node openclaw.mjs gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --token test-token-123 --port 19001 &
    sleep 8
```

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `aa77669d`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash -c "..."
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v4) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: aa77669d

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  234 passed (234)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 ✓ attempt.session-lock.test.ts (47 tests)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  47 passed (47)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  157 passed (157)

--- Test 4: SDK transport paths (no global maxRetries) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  26 passed (26)

--- Test 5: Full regression (all affected suites) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  83 passed (83)
 Test Files  2 passed (2)    Tests  26 passed (26)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  157 passed (157)
 Test Files  2 passed (2)    Tests  281 passed (281)
 passed 4 Vitest shards in 19.16s

--- Test 6: End-to-end transport retry suppression proof ---
Loopback test: mock server returns retryable 500, maxRetries:0 forwarded
through full completions transport → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)

--- Test 7: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions now accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1836:  maxRetries?: number,
1837:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {

All 3 call sites forward options?.maxRetries:
1933:  options?.maxRetries,
2373:  options?.maxRetries,
2603:  buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1075:  innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...options });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 547 tests pass across 6 affected test suites (4 Vitest shards) in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: Loopback regression test starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn()` path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user concurrent session test with an actual ECONNRESET during an embedded prompt with concurrent steering input was run. The proof relies on unit tests (including an end-to-end loopback transport test with a real HTTP server) that simulate the session-lock race, fingerprint mismatch, transport retry suppression, and error classification paths in a real Docker container environment.

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 234 |
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 47 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 157 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` | 18 |
| `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 9 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |

New test coverage includes:
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined
- `maxRetries`-only call returns an object (not `undefined`)
- Loopback end-to-end test: mock 500 server + `maxRetries: 0` → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)
- `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` default behavior (no caller options → maxRetries:0)
- Explicit `maxRetries` override preserved over embedded default
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- User-facing resend message for pure session takeover
- Preserved prompt error unwrapping from cleanup takeover
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter

## Changed Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`

Fixes #87180

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Two visible group-chat messages arriving against the same terminal (`failed`) session snapshot could race: the second turn force-clears the first turn's freshly admitted recovery operation, dropping the first message — the exact silent-loss path this PR fixes.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `b2b7996945`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: The two-turn race regression test (`does not kill a sibling recovery turn when a second visible turn races the same terminal snapshot`) was authored as part of this PR and does not exist on upstream/main. When run against the original commit (`ac79b730a1`) without the guard fix, the second turn force-fails the first recovery operation (the `terminalRecovery` guard did not exist).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts \
    src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
   ✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts (155 tests) 582ms
   ✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts (97 tests) 288ms
   ✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts (40 tests) 217ms
   ✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts (191 tests) 6650ms
   Test Files  4 passed (4)
        Tests  483 passed (483)

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts
   ✓ channels src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts (29 tests) 16ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  29 passed (29)

  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 15.52s
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All 512 tests pass across 2 Vitest shards, including the new two-turn concurrent recovery race regression test. The `terminalRecovery` flag prevents the second visible turn from force-clearing the first turn's in-flight recovery operation; both turns produce replies without message loss.
- **What was not tested**: No live channel (Telegram/Octo/Discord) end-to-end test was performed. The race condition is validated through focused Vitest integration tests that simulate concurrent visible inbound dispatches against shared terminal session state. The fix is channel-generic and does not depend on any channel-specific behavior.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: fix(install): trap SIGINT so Ctrl+C exits cleanly during upgrade doctor
target.body:
## Problem

When running `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, pressing Ctrl+C during `openclaw doctor` in the upgrade flow does not abort the installer. The SIGINT kills the doctor subprocess but the installer catches it as a regular failure, retries the npm install, continues past the prompt, and opens a browser to an unconfigured dashboard URL.

A deeper issue exists for the **interactive doctor** path (Clack-based prompts): Clack catches SIGINT internally, calls `guardCancel`, which calls `runtime.exit(0)`. This means the doctor process exits with code 0 (success), the installer marks `doctor_ok=1`, and proceeds with plugin updates and dashboard launch, even though the user pressed Ctrl+C.

## Fix

1. **Add SIGINT/SIGTERM trap**: `abort_install_int`/`abort_install_term` handlers show a warning and exit 130/143
2. **Propagate doctor exit code 130**: capture `openclaw doctor` exit code separately; if 130 (SIGINT), call `abort_install_int` instead of treating it as a regular failure
3. **Fix `guardCancel` exit code**: change `runtime.exit(0)` to `runtime.exit(130)` in `src/commands/onboard-helpers.ts` so Clack-caught Ctrl+C exits with the correct SIGINT convention code, not success
4. **Add SIGINT flag defense**: before running the interactive doctor, temporarily swap the INT trap to set a `_got_sigint_during_doctor` flag. After doctor exits, check the flag. This catches the foreground-child case where the child catches SIGINT and exits 0, as an additional defense layer on top of the exit code fix
5. **Conditional dashboard open**: `should_open_dashboard` is set to true ONLY when `run_doctor` succeeds (returns 0). A failed or interrupted doctor never triggers the browser open. The flag is also reset before the final interactive doctor so a failed final doctor does not inherit an earlier migration doctor success.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Pressing Ctrl+C (SIGINT) during an interactive doctor prompt where Clack catches SIGINT and exits 0 no longer marks `doctor_ok=1` and no longer proceeds with plugin updates or dashboard launch. The `guardCancel` function in `onboard-helpers.ts` now exits 130 instead of 0, and the installer has a belt-and-suspenders SIGINT flag defense for the foreground-child case.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 15.5 (Apple Silicon), bash 5.x (Homebrew), Node v24.15.0, npm 11.14.1, OpenClaw 2026.5.22 installed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Shell harness sourcing install.sh with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1`, simulating the exact interactive-doctor code path. Test 1: a foreground child that ignores SIGINT (like Clack doctor) and exits 0, while sending SIGINT to the parent shell during child execution. Tests 2-4: exit-130, clean exit-0, and exit-1 paths. Test 5-6: static grep verification of `guardCancel` source.

**Evidence after fix**: Shell harness test output, 6/6 pass:

```console
$ bash /tmp/test-sigint-doctor-gate.sh

=== Test 1: Foreground child exits 0 after catching SIGINT ===
    (simulates Clack doctor catching Ctrl+C and exiting 0)
  result: ABORT
  PASS: SIGINT during doctor triggers abort (expected=ABORT)

=== Test 2: Doctor exits 130 (killed by SIGINT) ===
  result: ABORT
  PASS: exit-130 doctor triggers abort (expected=ABORT)

=== Test 3: Normal doctor completion (exit 0, no SIGINT) ===
  result: doctor_ok=1 should_open_dashboard=true
  PASS: normal doctor marks success (expected=doctor_ok=1 should_open_dashboard=true)

=== Test 4: Doctor failure (exit 1) ===
  result: doctor_ok=0 should_open_dashboard=false
  PASS: failed doctor stays false (expected=doctor_ok=0 should_open_dashboard=false)

=== Test 5: guardCancel exits 130 (not 0) on cancellation ===
  PASS: guardCancel uses exit(130) (expected=1)
  PASS: guardCancel no longer uses exit(0) (expected=0)

=== Summary: 6/6 passed, 0 failed ===
ALL TESTS PASSED
```

SIGINT during upgrade doctor phase (real upgrade on macOS with OpenClaw installed):

```console
$ timeout --signal=INT 10 bash scripts/install.sh --install-method npm < /dev/null 2>&1; echo "EXIT=$?"

  OpenClaw Installer

Detected: macos
Install plan: macos, npm

[1/3] Preparing environment
Node.js v24.15.0 found

[2/3] Installing OpenClaw
OpenClaw installed

[3/3] Finalizing setup
OpenClaw installed successfully (2026.5.22)!
Config already present; running doctor
Running doctor to migrate settings
Running doctor

! Installation interrupted
EXIT=124
```

**Observed result after fix**: Test 1 (the foreground-child SIGINT case, which is the exact scenario from the ClawSweeper finding) correctly triggers ABORT instead of proceeding with `doctor_ok=1`. The `guardCancel` exit code change ensures doctor exits 130 on Clack cancellation (not 0), and the installer's SIGINT flag provides defense-in-depth. SIGINT during any phase including the doctor phase triggers `abort_install_int`, prints "Installation interrupted", and exits 130 (124 when wrapped by timeout). No dashboard opens.

**What was not tested**: Full interactive install with a real TTY Ctrl+C during a live Clack doctor prompt (requires manual TTY interaction with real Clack prompts rendered). SIGTERM delivery (tested SIGINT only). Live CDN `curl | bash` path.

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts (npm update, crash, systemd restart), any
messages that arrived while an agent turn was in progress are silently lost.
The followup queue lives only in process memory — there is no recovery path.

This is particularly noticeable on channels that serialize messages (e.g.
Telegram in queue mode): a message received during an active turn is queued
for delivery, then gone if the gateway restarts before that turn completes.

## Solution

Add a JSON persistence layer for the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global map.

### New file: `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.ts`

- `persistFollowupQueues()` — serializes all non-empty queue entries to
  `<stateDir>/live-chat-followup-queues.json`. Respects `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`
  via the existing `resolveStateDir()` utility. State file is deleted when all
  queues drain to empty.
- `restoreFollowupQueues()` — reads the state file at module init and repopulates
  `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` before any enqueue or drain fires.
- `PersistedFollowupRun` — a `Pick<FollowupRun, ...>` type that explicitly
  excludes runtime-only, non-serializable fields: `abortSignal`,
  `currentTurnContext`, `deliveryCorrelations`, `queuedLifecycle`. These are
  transient and must not survive a restart.

### Mutation hooks

`persistFollowupQueues()` is called after every state-changing operation:

| Site | File |
|---|---|
| `enqueueFollowupRun` — after `items.push` | `enqueue.ts` |
| Drain `finally` block — both empty-queue delete and reschedule branches | `drain.ts` |
| `clearFollowupQueue` — after `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.delete` | `state.ts` |
| `refreshQueuedFollowupSession` — after the run-rewrite loop | `state.ts` |

### Tests

`persist.test.ts` covers:
- State file written when queue has items
- State file deleted when all queues are empty
- Full round-trip: enqueue → persist → simulated restart → restore → verify prompt + routing fields
- Runtime-only `abortSignal` field not present after restore
- No-op when state file does not exist
- Malformed entries (non-array items, null key) are skipped

## Notes

- The `draining: false` invariant is enforced on restore — any in-flight drain
  that was interrupted is restarted by the normal drain scheduler on next enqueue.
- `summarySources` (contains `FollowupRun` objects, not serializable) is reset to
  `[]` on restore. `summaryLines` (plain strings) is persisted and restored.
- No schema migration needed for v1; unrecognized fields are ignored on restore.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Queued followup messages received during an active agent turn survive a gateway restart and redeliver exactly once. This head (`3ca1f5a78`) also persists `originatingReplyToId` and `inputProvenance` so restored runs keep reply-target context.
- Real environment tested: Local dev checkout on Linux (`jewseppi/openclaw`, branch `feat/queue-followup-persistence`), Node 24, real temp state dir via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, real on-disk `live-chat-followup-queues.json` I/O (no in-memory mocks for persist/restore).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  cd openclaw
  pnpm exec vitest run \
    src/auto-reply/reply/queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output (live module + real filesystem):
  ```
  RUN  v4.1.7 /home/joseph/dev/openclaw

   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  19 passed (19)
   Duration  2.18s

  Covered behaviors on head 3ca1f5a78:
  - Telegram-routed followup queued mid-turn → persisted to disk → simulated gateway restart → restore → exactly-once redelivery → no replay after second restart
  - originatingReplyToId + inputProvenance round-trip through persist+restore
  - state file mode 0o600, atomic write, auth profile selectors preserved, secret-bearing config stripped
  ```
- Observed result after fix: After simulated restart, restored queue items retain prompt, Telegram routing, reply target, and input provenance; drain delivers the restored prompt once; post-drain persistence prevents replay on a second restart.
- What was not tested: Full live native Telegram desktop capture (Mantis run failed on shared QA infra). Contributor-side live Telegram E2E remains blocked by gateway/session serialization constraints documented in prior PR comments.

### Storage note (ClawSweeper P1)

This PR intentionally uses a named v1 sidecar (`live-chat-followup-queues.json`) for crash-recovery of in-memory followup queues. A SQLite/shared-state migration is a larger follow-up; this patch keeps the artifact private (0o600), atomic, and minimal (allowlisted fields only). Happy to move to shared-state DB if maintainers prefer that shape before merge.
